---
title: "nitrix.stats.inference.multiple_comparisons"
generated: true
signature-hash: 44a788242deb6fb4
description: "Multiple-comparison corrections + multi-contrast combination for mass-univariate p-value / statistic maps."
listing:
  id: nitrix-stats-inference-multiple_comparisons-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-stats-inference-multiple_comparisons/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.stats.inference.multiple_comparisons`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Multiple-comparison corrections + multi-contrast combination for mass-univariate
p-value / statistic maps.

Companions to the permutation engine: false-discovery-rate control --
Benjamini-Hochberg (`fdr_bh`; valid under independence / PRDS),
Benjamini-Yekutieli (`fdr_by`; valid under *arbitrary* dependence), and
Storey's pi0-adaptive q-values (`fdr_storey`; higher power), behind a unified
`fdr(method=...)` dispatcher -- the Bonferroni family-wise correction, and the
**valid conjunction** (minimum-statistic) combination across contrasts (audit
N7).  All are pure array ops over a flat per-element p-value vector.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `n` | query points, each an `ndim`-vector coordinate (variadic) |
| `k` | kernel or basis components |
| `*spatial` | the spatial axes of a grid (variadic) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-stats-inference-multiple_comparisons-symbols}
:::
